title: Uniface 10: Changes in Command Line Switches
breadcrumbs: What's New in Uniface 10 Compared to Uniface 9
Uniface 10: Changes in Command Line Switches
text: Uniface 10: Changes in Command Line Switches Changes have been made to the functionality of some command line switches and sub-switches Table 1. New Command Line Switches and Sub-switches Switch Description /cmi Sub-switch that determines whether compiled module information is generated by the command line compiler. /genSql Switch used to generate SQL command scripts for preparing application databases and the Uniface Repository. /iap Specify whether to compile all development objects, regardless of their purpose (Normal, Palettes, and Templates). /ilv Compile the specified components using the indicated severity level for the compiler message 1000 - #Include IncludeScript not found /plt Sub-switch that compiles main development objects that are defined as palettes, but does not generate runtime objects. /tpl Sub-switch that compiles main development objects that are defined as templates, but does not generate runtime objects. Table 2. Changed Command Line Switches and Sub-switches Switch Description /cpy It is not possible to use the Data Copy facilities such as /cpy and $ude("copy") to import Repository data. Use the export and import functionality instead. For more information, see Data Copy Facility and Repository Versioning . /imp It is not possible to use the Repository import facilities /imp and $ude("import") to import data created by the Data Copy facilities such as /cpy and $ude("copy") . For more information, see Export and Import Facilities /ceo /con (and /anm) /cpt /dsp /esv /frm /rpt /ssv /svc /usp These command line switches now support the /tpl and /plt sub-switches. Command line switches that compile components also support /cmi . Table 3. Obsolete Command Line Switches Switch Description /exe Switch that was specific to the Development Environment. No longer supported. /ins Switch used to import the meta dictionary in Uniface 9. In Uniface 10, you can import the umeta.xml as you would any other Uniface sources. /rma /sym The Uniface Deployment Environment (URMA) is no longer delivered with Uniface, so this switch is now obsolete. You can use the Uniface Resource Manager ( urm ) to do most of the tasks that /rma handled. For more information, see urm . Cross-reference functionality is not (yet) implemented in Uniface 10, so the /sym is not supported. Was this topic helpful? Like Dislike
